name: Suitability Review Required
description: SEC Rule 2111 — All investment recommendations must be suitable for the client based on their investment profile, risk tolerance, financial situation, and investment objectives.
triggerCondition: Any rebalancing or new investment recommendation
action: Verify client's current risk tolerance profile is up to date. Confirm that proposed changes align with documented investment objectives. Document suitability rationale before executing trades.

name: Concentration Risk Disclosure
description: SEC guidance on concentrated positions — Advisors must disclose and document risks when a single sector exceeds 25% of portfolio value or a single holding exceeds 10%.
triggerCondition: Sector allocation exceeds 25% or single holding exceeds 10% of portfolio value
action: Provide written disclosure to client about concentration risk. Document client acknowledgment. Consider recommending diversification strategy.

name: Best Execution Obligation
description: SEC Rule 5310 — Broker must use reasonable diligence to ascertain best market for the security and buy/sell at the most favorable price under prevailing conditions.
triggerCondition: Any trade execution across all recommended changes
action: Document price comparison across available venues. Ensure execution during optimal market hours. Record best execution analysis for audit trail.

name: High-Risk Rebalance Approval
description: Company Policy CP-401 — Portfolio rebalancing involving changes exceeding 15% of total portfolio value requires senior advisor approval and compliance sign-off.
triggerCondition: Proposed trades exceed 15% of total portfolio value in aggregate
action: Submit rebalancing proposal to senior advisor for review. Obtain compliance department sign-off before executing. Document approval chain in client file.

name: Restricted Product Check
description: Company Policy CP-210 — Certain investment products are on the restricted list and require additional due diligence or are prohibited for certain client profiles.
triggerCondition: Recommendation includes leveraged ETFs, inverse products, options strategies, or products on the restricted list
action: Cross-reference recommendations against current restricted product list. If flagged, substitute with approved alternative or escalate to compliance for exception review.

name: Client Communication Standards
description: Company Policy CP-305 — All client communications regarding investment recommendations must include standard disclaimers, risk disclosures, and cannot guarantee returns.
triggerCondition: Any client email, letter, or communication containing investment recommendations
action: Ensure email includes standard risk disclosure footer. Verify no return guarantees or promissory language. Include 'past performance is not indicative of future results' disclaimer.

name: Anti-Churning Compliance
description: SEC Rule 15c1-7 — Advisors must not engage in excessive trading that primarily generates commissions without benefiting the client.
triggerCondition: Multiple rebalancing actions within a 90-day window or portfolio turnover exceeding 40%
action: Review trade frequency over the past 90 days. Calculate portfolio turnover ratio. Document investment rationale for each trade. Ensure fee impact is disclosed to client.

name: Concentration Threshold Policy
description: Company Policy CP-115 — No single sector should exceed 40% of a client portfolio without documented client consent and risk acknowledgment form.
triggerCondition: Any sector allocation exceeds 40% of total portfolio value
action: Flag portfolio for concentration review. Obtain signed client risk acknowledgment form (Form RA-40). Recommend diversification plan. Schedule quarterly review.
